data.title: Justin Sun transferred $9 million worth of unlocked WLFI into HTX
data.description: According to Bubblemaps, Justin Sun transferred $9 million in unlocked WLFI to HTX. Over the past three days, he has transferred a total of $10 million to centralized exchanges. Earlier news broke that World Liberty Financial (WLFI) had blacklisted addresses associated with Justin Sun, implicating approximately 540 million unlocked tokens and 2.4 billion locked tokens. The project stated that it suspected exchanges were using user tokens for sell-offs, thereby depressing the token's price.

data.title: South Korea Caps Crypto Lending at 20% Interest, Bans Over-Collateralized Loans
data.description:
South Korea's Financial Services Commission implemented comprehensive crypto lending guidelines capping interest at 20% annually and banning over-collateralized loans after 27,600 investors borrowed 1. 5 trillion won ($1. 1 billion) in one month, with 13% facing forced liquidation due to market volatility.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/south-korea-caps-crypto-lending-at-20-interest-bans-over-collateralized-loans/

source: https://cryptopotato.com/big-btc-breakout-brewing-bitcoin-prepares-for-ath-retest/

data.title: BlackRock dwarfs competitors with $150 million Ethereum purchase in a day
data.description:
BlackRock’s dominance of the spot Ethereum (ETH) exchange-traded fund (ETF) was on display on September 4, when the world’s largest investment firm dwarfed competitors in the market. In this case, BlackRock recorded $148.8 million in net Ethereum ETF inflows, making it the sole major buyer and far outpacing rivals, according to data retrieved by Finbold from Coinglass. In contrast, several competitors faced heavy redemptions
source: https://finbold.com/blackrock-dwarfs-competitors-with-150-million-ethereum-purchase-in-a-day/

source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/thumzup-1m-in-bitcoin-ether-doge-xrp-solana/

data.title: South Korea rolls out strict guidelines for crypto lending services
data.description:
5-9-2025 – South Korea’s Financial Services Commission has introduced new regulations for cryptocurrency lending on centralized exchanges, effective immediately. The guidelines, detailed in an FSC press release, outline the boundaries of lending operations by drawing on international examples and emphasizing user safeguards. Key restrictions include banning loans that leverage beyond collateral value and imposing a [.
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/south-korea-rolls-out-strict-guidelines-for-crypto-lending-services/

data.title: Solv Protocol Expands BTC+ Vault Capacity Amid High Demand
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Solv Protocol has announced that its structured yield vault, BTC+, has reached its initial capacity of 400 BTC in less than a month. In response to this demand, Solv has increased the total capacity of the BTC+ vault to 1000 BTC and introduced a Boost Rewards mechanism. The next phase of the project will include new features and optimizations to advance its 'Bitcoin Unbound' strategy.

Official data indicates that the BTC+ vault offers an annualized yield of approximately 5%, which is higher than the industry average. The vault is integrated across more than eight blockchains and utilizes proof of reserves and Chainlink SER to demonstrate yield performance. The project has successfully completed two redemption cycles.

data.title: Listed company Robot Consulting plans to invest 1 billion yen in ETH to promote legal technology development
data.description: Publicly listed company Robot Consulting announced plans to invest up to 1 billion yen in ETH between the fourth quarter of 2025 and spring 2026. The company aims to promote the technological advancement of legal services by integrating blockchain technology with legal tech and to build a digital asset reserve. Robot Consulting plans to utilize Ethereum across multiple business areas, leveraging the blockchain ecosystem and smart contract functionality, to enhance its influence in the legal tech sector. (Globe Newswire)

data.title: Gemini Launches ETH and SOL Staking and Perpetual Contract Services in the EU
data.description: Crypto exchange Gemini has announced the launch of ETH and Solana staking services in the European Union, with no minimum deposit requirement, annualized rewards of up to 6% for Solana and floating returns for ETH. The exchange also introduced USDC-denominated perpetual contracts with up to 100x leverage and no expiration date. This initiative builds on Gemini's MiCA compliance approval. (CoinDesk)

data.title: A newly created address hoarded 15,000 ETH, worth $65.84 million
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the new address 0xAF8 has accumulated 15,256.6 ETH, valued at $65.84 million. This address has withdrawn 15,256.6 ETH from OKX over the past 11 hours at an average price of $4,315, resulting in a current profit of $1.296 million.

data.title: Research Initiative Launched on Global Stablecoin Risk Governance
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the National Natural Science Foundation of China's Management Science Department has opened applications for the third phase of the 2025 emergency management project titled 'Research on Global Stablecoin Risk Governance and Cross-Border Collaborative Regulatory Systems.' The deadline for applications is October 9, 2025, at 16:00 UTC+8. The research period is set for 10 to 12 months, from November 2025 to October 2026.

The project comprises one main topic and five subtopics. The main topic focuses on the evolution trends of global stablecoins, risk transmission, and China's risk governance and strategic response. Subtopic 1 examines the structural evolution of global stablecoins and their impact on the international monetary system. Subtopic 2 investigates the inherent vulnerabilities of stablecoins, risk transmission, and macroprudential management. Subtopic 3 explores the effects of stablecoins on monetary sovereignty and the effectiveness of monetary policy. Subtopic 4 addresses the impact of digital currencies on China's financial intermediary system, including transmission and response strategies. Subtopic 5 studies the regulatory framework for global stablecoins, financial infrastructure, and cross-border collaboration.

The National Natural Science Foundation of China established the Management Science Department's emergency management project in 1997. This initiative primarily funds research that uses scientific methods to address urgent and critical issues in national macro-management and development strategies, as well as 'hot' and 'difficult' problems in economic, technological, and social development practices.

data.title: Tristero Research Warns RWA Tokenization Could Trigger ‘On-Chain Subprime Crisis’
data.description:
The tokenized real-world assets market has surged to $26. 5 billion following 70% growth in 2025, yet new research warns that rapid expansion could trigger an "on-chain subprime crisis" through dangerous mismatches between illiquid physical assets and hyper-fast blockchain markets.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/tristero-research-warns-rwa-tokenization-could-trigger-on-chain-subprime-crisis/

data.title: LBank and CertiK Skynet form a strategic partnership
data.description: LBank has officially announced a strategic partnership with CertiK Skynet. As a centralized exchange integrating CertiK Skynet, LBank will leverage the platform to enhance its security, transparency, and real-time risk monitoring capabilities, creating a more secure and trusted trading environment for users. CertiK Skynet is a real-time, automated security assessment system for Web3 projects, exchanges, and wallets, offering independent review and risk monitoring capabilities.
